name: Check Into Cash
description_short: Check Into Cash offers payday loans up to $600, title loans, and financial services with same-day funding at 800+ locations including Ferndale, MI.
description_long:
Check Into Cash has operated for 30 years as a storefront lender providing short-term financial solutions to customers across the United States. The company specializes in emergency cash products designed for immediate needs, with a physical presence in communities including a Ferndale, Michigan location at 749 East 9 Mile Road.

The company's primary offerings include payday loans (up to $600 available in-store and online), title loans using vehicle titles as collateral, installment loans with longer terms, and a Flex Line of Credit for flexible borrowing. Beyond lending, Check Into Cash provides check cashing services (accepting checks others won't), Western Union money transfers, bill payment processing, Green Dot Visa Debit Card accounts, and tax preparation services. Applications require a Social Security number, government-issued photo ID, proof of an active checking account with routing number, and proof of income.

The Ferndale location operates Monday-Saturday with extended hours (8am-7pm Friday, 9am-7pm most weekdays, 9am-4pm Saturday) and maintains a physical storefront presence, differentiating it from online-only lenders. The company emphasizes confidentiality, quick processing, and same-day cash disbursement upon approval. They disclose that loan amounts and eligibility depend on ability to repay and underwriting criteria, with restrictions, rules, and fees varying by location and state.

As a traditional payday and title lender, Check Into Cash carries the inherent caveat that these products typically involve high fees and APRs typical of the emergency lending industry. The company's 30-year history and multi-state operation indicate stability, but consumers should carefully review state-specific rates, fees, and terms before applying, as these products are designed for genuine emergencies rather than ongoing financial needs.
pros: Same-day cash funding available for approved applicants who can walk out with money
Physical storefront locations provide in-person service and consultations, not phone/online only
Accepts check cashing for checks that other providers won't accept
Multiple loan products (payday, installment, title, Flex Line) provide borrowing flexibility
30-year operating history suggests established business practices and community presence
Extended retail hours including Friday 8am-7pm and Saturday service for working customers
Transparent disclosure of application requirements and underwriting criteria upfront
cons: Payday loans up to $600 cap is low for emergencies exceeding that threshold
Website provides no state-specific rates or fee information to compare costs before visiting
Title loans risk vehicle repossession if borrower cannot repay, per standard industry practice
No clear disclosure of APR ranges or typical fees for different loan products on location page
Loan approval not guaranteed; website states 'not all consumers will qualify' without pre-qualification tools
best_for: Workers with unexpected emergencies needing $100-$600 before next paycheck
People with fair/poor credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit cards
Customers preferring in-person service and same-day cash over online lending platforms
Individuals who need check cashing or bill payment services in addition to emergency funds
diagnosis: Check Into Cash is best for employed individuals facing genuine short-term cash emergencies who can access a physical storefront and repay by their next paycheck. The main caveat is that payday and title loans carry high costs typical of emergency lending; consumers should exhaust other options (employer advances, credit lines, family loans, credit union PALs under 36% APR) before using these products, and must verify state-specific rates and fees before committing.
services: Title loans using vehicle titles as collateral
Installment loans with extended repayment terms
Flex Line of Credit for revolving credit access
Check cashing including checks declined elsewhere
Green Dot Visa Debit Card accounts
In-store loan applications with same-day funding
